core.when: When computing forward returns from price data
core.action: use shift(-period) to correctly align future prices with current factor values

consequence.description: Using shift(period) instead of shift(-period) introduces look-ahead bias, causing future information to be used in historical analysis and producing backtests that cannot be replicated in live trading

core.when: When preparing historical data for backtesting
core.action: Verify startup candle count equals or exceeds longest indicator lookback period

consequence.description: Insufficient startup candles cause indicators to reference future data, introducing look-ahead bias that inflates backtest performance

core.when: When computing rolling volatility and Sharpe ratio
core.action: annualize volatility using sqrt(APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR) where APPROX_BDAYS_PER_YEAR equals 252

consequence.description: Annualized volatility and Sharpe ratio will be incorrectly scaled if using calendar days instead of trading days

core.when: When implementing volatility targeting with rolling window periods
core.action: account for NaN values in the first N periods before the rolling window completes

consequence.description: Volatility-adjusted leverage will be NaN for the initial periods equal to the rolling window (vol_periods), causing backtest P&L series to contain NaN values for warmup periods and potentially causing downstream calculation errors in portfolio aggregation

evidence_refs.summary: src/finmarketpy/backtest/backtestengine.py:2875-2876 explicitly sets lev_df[0:vol_periods] = np.nan to ignore elements before the vol window kicks in; rolling_volatility at line 2848 requires vol_peri

core.when: When preparing datasets for machine learning model training and validation
core.action: Split data temporally into TRAIN, VALID, TEST segments using date range boundaries — verify each TRAIN timestamps precede VALID, which precedes TEST

consequence.description: Random or incorrect temporal splitting introduces look-ahead bias where future information leaks into training data, causing backtest results to significantly overestimate live trading performance

core.when: When implementing data ingestion to data filtering data flow
core.action: Maintain chronological order with no temporal gaps in OHLCV bar timestamps

consequence.description: Backtest equity curve exhibits look-ahead bias when non-chronological data causes indicator values to reference future prices, leading to live trading returns that fall far below backtested results
